attacking_style: Favors long-range, elevation-focused attacks on Cat-1 climbs, often exploiting descents after summit efforts. Shows willingness to go early in breakaways rather than waiting for final-climb sprints.
team_tactics: Often operates with limited dedicated support; when racing for GC carries defense burden himself. Has been subordinated to co-leaders (Uran/Carthy 2019), suggesting mid-tier protection priority even when in good form.
terrain_preference: Excels on sustained Cat-1 climbs with technical descents to follow. Shows unexpected competence in crosswinds and classics-style racing, but elevation-specific strength is primary weapon.
vulnerability: Insufficient explosive power to create separation when marked by top-tier GC riders. Gets dropped when teammates set tempo for co-leaders, revealing climbing ceiling below pure Grand Tour contenders.
key_patterns: Responds immediately to GC attacks on climbs, finishing on podium multiple times (2019, 2022).
Exploits descents tactically after climb efforts to gain separation from pursuers (Vuelta 2019 win).
Initiates or joins early breakaways on mountain stages rather than conserving energy (Catalunya, Vuelta).
Shows GC ambition through bonus sprint collection despite being climber specialist (Vuelta 2019).
Gets marked and neutralized quickly when attempting moves with stronger teams (UAE Amstel 2022).
form_trajectory: neutral: 2022 showed podium finishes and aggressive racing at Catalunya, but Amstel marked as insufficient threat. Gap since observations leaves current trajectory unclear.
